The '''''Dagger''-class starfighter''' was a fighter craft designed by [[Republic Sienar Systems/Legends|Republic Sienar Systems]] in the last century of the Republic and produced in large numbers around the time of the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:Dagger_swsf.jpg|thumb|left|150px|A head-on view of a ''Dagger''.]]
The ''Dagger'' [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] had a triple wing design. The wings were pointed and knife-like in appearance, During flight, the whole craft would rotate around the cockpit. The ''Dagger'' was armed with three [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] which were located on the hull, next to where the wings connected.

The fighter was small, fast, maneuverable, had powerful weaponry, and was relatively cheap and easy to obtain. However, it lacked a life support system, or [[Acceleration compensator/Legends|inertial dampeners]], this dramatically increased the risk of the pilot suffering the effect of extreme [[Gravity/Legends|G-Forces]] when quickly accelerating or turning. As such a Pilot had to rely on his own skill (and in no small part the quality of his space suit) in order to survive a battle.<ref name="TCWCG" />

==History==
The fighters were produced by the thousands over the decades prior to the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. The majority were purchased by planetary defense forces, while many others seemingly found their way into the hands of [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenary]] organizations.<ref name="TCWCG" /> ''Daggers'' were also used in an assault on a Naboo [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] convoy above Naboo.<ref name="SW:SF" /> In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]] happens in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> [[Rhys Dallows]] and [[Vana Sage]] were forced to [[Skirmish at Eos|contend]] with these craft during their attempts to protect [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]; the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] had recruited a mercenary organization whose jobs involved such things as an [[Attempted kidnapping of Queen Amidala|attempted kidnapping]] of [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Amidala]]. ''Daggers'' made up the first three waves of the mercenary assault force during the kidnapping attempt.<ref name="SW:SF">[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]</ref>

With the advent of the Clone Wars, numerous ''Daggers'' were also found in the planetary fleets of Separatist worlds. The production of the ''Dagger'' stopped with the rise of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] disbanding of Republic Sienar. The successor company, [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]], borrowed a number of elements from the ''Dagger'' in their initial design of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]]. The design was later used for as the basis for the [[V38 assault fighter]] and the [[TIE/ph "Phantom" Multi-Role Stealth Starfighter/Legends|TIE/ph phantom]].

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Dagger.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Game concept art.]]
''Dagger'' Starfighters first appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']]. Daggers also appeared in the game's [[2002]] sequel, ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Starfighter]]. ''In this game, they only appear in the bonus missions.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] {{1st}}
